description: A candid color photograph (1200x800) of a CBP Office of Field Operations officer processing a traveler at what appears to be an airport or port-of-entry inspection booth. In sharp focus is a male officer with short graying hair wearing a white N95 respirator mask and a dark CBP uniform; his black vest reads 'CONROY' on a name tape above a large patch 'CBP / FIELD OPERATIONS / FEDERAL OFFICER', and he wears a CBP credential badge, blue nitrile gloves, and has a pen in his vest. He is gesturing toward a person seen from behind in the foreground (out of focus, wearing a dark jacket). The background shows a fluorescent-lit inspection hall with glass partitions and another uniformed officer. The lighting and composition are documentary and photojournalistic in style. There is no graphic-design overlay, slogan, headline, or added agency branding on the image (only the uniform patch text appears).
notable_text: (uniform text only: CONROY; CBP FIELD OPERATIONS FEDERAL OFFICER) - no added slogan
